provenance.source_corpus: Tallgrass prairie restoration: implications of increased atmospheric nitrogen deposition when site preparation minimizes adventive grasses

source_spans.text: We used a field experiment to examine how N addition affected species composition and plant productivity
Total percent cover (i.e. percent cover values summed over all species) increased with N addition
significant increase in non-leguminous native forbs with N addition
driven by increases in the cover of Conyza canadensis
native leguminous forbs were significantly lower in the N addition plots than the control plots
significant increase in total native C3 grass cover in response to N addition
no significant N addition effects on total native C4 grass cover
adventive grasses remained mostly absent from the plots
no significant effects of N addition on total aboveground biomass
no significant effects of N addition on the Shannon diversity index
no significant effects of N addition on... species richness
no significant effects of N addition on... species evenness
Site preparation designed to exhaust the soil seedbank of adventive species can improve the success of tallgrass prairie restoration
